reason: SearchResult became a behavior-bearing frozen dataclass (quarry-gcnf): the result_similarity free function was deleted and method_ratio restored to 1.0. avg_params (0->0.86) and classes_per_module (1->2) rose as the natural shape of adding a proper domain class with methods, both well within absolute thresholds (4.0 / 3). avg_complexity improved 3.0->1.29. Recording the genuine improvement, not laundering a regression.

Added __post_init__ distance-range invariant guard (quarry-gcnf): avg_params improved 0.86->0.75 (the guard is a zero-param method), avg_complexity ticked up 1.29->1.38 as the natural cost of one validation branch, still far below the 10 threshold. method_ratio holds at 1.0; module_size 173 well under 300. Recording a net hardening improvement, not laundering a regression.

security-mandated fail-open wrap (design 3c) + SF-2 committed-not-pushed logging; 6-method module cannot add boundary logic without avg_complexity 1.6->2.0 / avg_params 0.4->0.43, both far under the 5.0/4.0 absolute limits; _read_rss extraction registers as improved

carries Copilot-flagged aborted-vs-committed logging fix: ShadowSyncResult.unpushed_detail() + _push_and_warn mirror (+9 lines, one branch); module_size 216<<300, avg_complexity 2.57<<5.0; avg_params improved 1.08->1.07; paired with sync_finalize avg_complexity 2.0->1.86

run() docstring gained the required UnicodeDecodeError expected-failure clause for the fail-open security fix; file is otherwise pristine (all 11 metrics at target, avg_complexity 2.47) so no metric can honestly improve; module_size baseline corrected 235->236 to carry the mandated documentation, still far under the 300 threshold

GitRunner.run docstring gained the required note that text=True decodes stdout as strict UTF-8, so invalid git output raises UnicodeDecodeError now caught in the runner's never-raises contract (secondary fail-open vector); module_size baseline corrected 51->54 to carry the mandated documentation, still far under the 300 threshold

mandatory fail-closed parent-capture enumeration: parent_tracked_captures now raises on a git ls-files failure and _refusal_reason adds a try/except refusal branch, growing module_size past 300 (298->315) and max/avg complexity (+1 branch); code is already all A-grade with no cheap offset

mandatory fail-closed catch: _parent_tracked_leak now wraps repo.parent_tracked_captures in try/except RuntimeError to report an unverifiable-git state as a required doctor failure instead of crashing; the +1 branch nudges avg_complexity (4.60, still under 5.0) with no cheap offset in this small already-clean module

correctness fix: unpushed_detail now labels a committed-then-visibility-refused run 'committed but not pushed (<reason>)' instead of the wrong 'aborted before commit'; the committed-first precedence adds one 'or' branch (avg_complexity 2.47->2.53, still far under 5.0), a real correctness gain worth the +1

avg_params 0.33->0.38 is a denominator artifact of extracting the shared _ls_files fail-closed enumeration helper (3 params: runner, argv, label) into a ~23-method module; 0.38 is far under the 4.0 target. The same change genuinely improved module_size 315->314, avg_complexity 2.50->2.42, and classes_per_module 2->1 by extracting the Visibility enum to its own module.

byte-exact strip=False read option for GitRunner.run: the commit-time fixed-point gate must verify the exact committed blob bytes; adding the option raises avg_params (1.0->1.33) and module_size (54->59) on a 54-line file, both far below threshold; make check passes
